advice: For fever, take rest, drink clean water and fluids. Place a cool, damp cloth on the forehead. If the fever is above 102°F or lasts more than 2 days, visit the nearest clinic.
For headache, rest in a quiet, dark room and drink water. Avoid loud noise and bright light. If the headache is severe, frequent, or with vomiting, seek medical help.
For cold and cough, drink warm fluids, take steam inhalation, and rest well. If cough lasts more than 1 week or has blood, consult a doctor immediately.
For mild stomach pain, drink clean water and eat light, bland food. Avoid spicy and oily items. If pain is severe, with vomiting or blood in stool, visit the nearest hospital.
For diarrhea, drink ORS or rice water to prevent dehydration. Avoid street food. If diarrhea lasts more than 2 days, or with blood, see a doctor.
For vomiting, sip small amounts of clean water or ORS. Avoid solid food until vomiting stops. If continuous or with blood, go to the hospital quickly.
For dehydration, drink ORS solution, coconut water, or lemon water with salt and sugar. Rest in a cool place. If very weak or unconscious, seek immediate medical care.
For minor cuts, wash the wound with clean water, apply antiseptic, and cover with a clean cloth. If bleeding does not stop or wound is deep, go to the nearest clinic.
Keep the person calm and still. Do not cut, suck, or apply herbs to the bite. Tie a cloth above the bite (not too tight). Take the patient to the hospital immediately.
Wash the bite with soap and water for 15 minutes. Cover with a clean cloth. Go to the hospital immediately for anti-rabies vaccine.
For small burns, hold under cool running water for 10 minutes. Do not apply toothpaste or oil. Cover with a clean cloth. For large burns, seek emergency care immediately.
For toothache, rinse with warm salt water. Avoid very hot or cold foods. If swelling or pain continues, consult a dentist.
For red or infected eye, wash with clean water. Do not rub the eye. If pain, swelling, or pus occurs, visit a doctor.
For breathing difficulty, sit upright, loosen tight clothes, and stay calm. If inhaler prescribed, use it. If breathing does not improve, call for emergency help immediately.
For pregnancy emergency or heavy bleeding, keep the mother calm and lying down. Do not give food or water. Take her to the nearest hospital immediately.
